home.subtitle: Kagibi encrypts your files on your device before any upload. Neither us nor anyone else can access them — mathematically. Hosted in France, GDPR-compliant, immune to the Cloud Act.
home.step1Desc: Your files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM directly in your browser. The key never leaves your device.
home.step2Desc: Only the encrypted stream reaches our servers in France. Even with full infrastructure access, no one can decrypt your files.
home.step3Desc: Only you can decrypt and read your data. Share securely via encrypted links or direct P2P transfer.
home.featSubtitle: A complete platform built on solid cryptographic foundations.
home.feat1Desc: Client-side AES-256-GCM encryption. Your keys never leave your device. Our servers only see encrypted noise — impossible otherwise.
home.feat2Desc: Send large files directly, browser to browser. No intermediate storage, maximum speed, no trace on our servers.
home.feat3Desc: 100% OVH France infrastructure (Gravelines). Outside US jurisdiction. Strict GDPR compliance. Your data never leaves Europe.
home.feat4Desc: Source code fully published under the AGPLv3 license. Audit yourself what Kagibi does with your data. Trust should not be blind.
home.ossPara1: Kagibi is published under the GNU AGPLv3 license — the strongest copyleft license. Any modification deployed in production must be shared publicly. No hidden proprietary version, no possible backdoor.
home.ossPara2: The code is freely auditable, forkable and improvable by everyone. Contribute to security, report bugs, suggest features — every Pull Request is welcome.
home.ctaSubtitle: Join Kagibi and store your files without ever compromising your privacy.

transfer.heroSubtitle: Send up to 10 GB directly, no upload. Maximum speed, zero trace.
transfer.step1Desc: Choose the files to send (10 GB max)
transfer.step2Desc: A secure link is generated instantly
transfer.step3Desc: Direct P2P connection via TURN server
transfer.feat1Desc: No server upload, direct P2P transfer at full bandwidth
transfer.feat2Desc: Your files are encrypted end-to-end during the transfer
transfer.feat3Desc: No file is stored on our servers, absolute peace of mind
transfer.feat4Desc: Service available at all times, no usage limits
transfer.ctaSubtitle: Sign in to start using instant P2P transfer
transfer.ctaNote: Free and unlimited for all users
transfer.tech1Desc: Peer-to-peer communication protocol standardized by the W3C. Enables a direct connection between browsers without intermediary.
transfer.tech2Desc: Relay infrastructure for connections that cannot be made directly. Guarantees a successful transfer even behind a strict NAT.
transfer.tech3Desc: Client-side encryption before transfer. Keys are exchanged via secure signaling, never stored.

compare.title: Kagibi vs. other solutions
compare.subtitle: Not all cloud storage solutions are equal. Here is why client-side encryption changes everything.
compare.disclaimer: Information collected publicly from official websites and vendor documentation. Last updated: April 2026.
compare.ctaTitle: The solution that mathematically cannot read your data
compare.ctaSubtitle: Client-side encryption is not an option — it is the foundation.
compare.r1tip: Your files are encrypted on your device before sending — the server only sees noise.
compare.r2tip: Direct browser-to-browser transfer, no intermediate storage.
compare.r5tip: Servers outside US jurisdiction cannot be compelled to hand over data.
compare.r8tip: Deploy your own instance on your infrastructure.
compare.r9tip: Prohibits proprietary forks — any modification must remain public.
compare.techProton5: Part of the source code is closed
compare.techNextcloud5: Experimental E2EE module, not enabled by default
compare.techGoogle1: Encryption in transit and at rest
compare.techGoogle2: Google holds and manages all keys
compare.techGoogle5: Content used to train AI models

security.heroSub: This is not a marketing promise — it is a mathematical constraint. Here is, concretely, what happens on your device and what the server never sees.
security.sec1Title: The server stores data it cannot read
security.sec1Sub: Every time you encrypt a file, all the cryptography happens in your browser, never on the server. The server only receives encrypted bytes. Without your password — which it does not know — it cannot do anything with them.
security.cmpBadNote: The server encrypts itself → it holds the key → it can read everything, hand it to authorities, get hacked.
security.cmpGoodNote: The server only receives encrypted data. Without your password, it is mathematically incapable of decrypting.
security.sec2Sub: All your data is protected by a chain of nested keys. Each key encrypts another — only your password can unlock everything.
security.sec3Title: What happens when you create an account
security.sec3Sub: All the cryptography is performed in your browser before a single byte is sent.
security.regStep1Desc: Your password is passed to Argon2id in a Web Worker. The result (KEK) never leaves the browser.
security.regStep2Desc: A random AES-256 key is generated via CSPRNG. It will be stored in the Service Worker with extractable: false.
security.regStep3Desc: The Master Key is encrypted (wrapped) with the KEK via AES-GCM. This ciphertext is sent to the server.
security.regStep4Desc: A 4096-bit RSA-OAEP key pair is generated. The public key goes to the server. The private key is encrypted by the Master Key.
security.regStep5Desc: 32 random bytes. A SHA-256 hash goes to the server for verification. The Master Key is also wrapped with this code.
security.regStep6Title: What is sent to the server
security.regStep6Desc: Only encrypted or public data. Never your password, the KEK, or the Master Key in plaintext.
security.sec4Sub: Files are split into 10 MB blocks, each encrypted independently with a unique nonce (deterministic by chunk number). Never any plaintext on the network.
security.uploadDone: ✓ {n} chunks encrypted and uploaded — no plaintext byte was transmitted
security.sec5Title: Sharing a file without the server seeing the key
security.sec5Sub: When Alice shares a file with Bob, the file key is encrypted with Bob's RSA public key. The server cannot decrypt it — only Bob, with his private key, can.
security.shareStep1Action: Retrieves her file key from memory
security.shareStep3Action: Encrypts the file key with Bob's public key
security.shareStep4Data: Cannot decrypt it (no Bob's private key)
security.shareStep5Action: Decrypts the file key with his RSA private key
security.shareStep5Data: RSA-OAEP.decrypt(encrypted_key, bob_private_key) → fileKey → decrypts the file
security.svServerNote: The server cannot decrypt anything — it holds neither Bob's private key nor Alice's Master Key.
security.sec6Title: The Master Key is never exposed to JavaScript
security.sec6Sub: Even if a malicious extension or an XSS vulnerability injects JavaScript into the page, it cannot steal your Master Key. Here is why.
security.swWorkerResponse:
✓ Uses the key to encrypt/decrypt
✗ NEVER exports it
security.swProp1Desc: The WebCrypto API forbids key export. crypto.subtle.exportKey() returns an error.
security.swProp2Desc: 30 minutes of inactivity → Master Key erased from the Service Worker → automatic logout.
security.swProp3Desc: A MutationObserver watches for scripts injected into the DOM. onerror handlers are blocked.
security.sec7Title: What the server sees — and does not see
security.sees7: File metadata (name encrypted if enabled, size, MIME)
security.never4: 🚫 The RSA private key in plaintext
security.ctaSubtitle: The full source code is available under the AGPLv3 license. Everything can be verified, audited, forked.
security.khPasswordTooltipBody: Passed to Argon2id in a separate Web Worker, it is never serialized, never sent to the server. The produced hash is the KEK.
security.khKekTooltipBody: Derived from your password via Argon2id (64 MB RAM, 4 passes). It is used solely to encrypt/decrypt the Master Key. It is never stored.
security.khMasterTooltipBody1: AES-256 key randomly generated at registration. Stored encrypted (by the KEK) on the server. In session in the Service Worker with
security.khRsaTooltipBody: Generated at registration, encrypted by the Master Key before being sent to the server. Used for sharing: the sender encrypts a file key with your public key.
security.khFolderTooltipBody: Each folder has its own AES-256 key, encrypted by the Master Key. Sharing a folder = sharing this key encrypted with the recipient's RSA public key.
security.khFileTooltipBody: Each file has its own AES-256 key, encrypted by the folder key. Enables key rotation and granular per-file revocation.
security.rfEncMkSub: Stored on the server · unusable without KEK

values.heroSub: Kagibi is not just a storage tool. It's a promise: that your data belongs to you — fully and permanently.
values.sec1Sub: These values are not words on a website. They are embedded in every line of our code.

v1.title: Privacy by design
v1.desc: Confidentiality is not a feature added as an afterthought. It is the fundamental architecture of Kagibi. Your password never leaves your device.

v2.title: Radical transparency
v2.desc: Our code is open source and auditable by anyone. We have nothing to hide — and we prove it by publishing every line on GitHub.

v3.title: Digital sovereignty
v3.desc: Your data belongs to you. Not to us. Not to advertisers. Not to governments. You are the sole master of your files.

v4.title: Security first
v4.desc: Every feature is designed with security in mind from the start — not as a layer added on top. Zero-knowledge by default, everywhere.

c1.title: End-to-end encryption
c1.desc: Your files are encrypted on your device before reaching us. AES-256-GCM. Nobody — including our team — can decrypt your data.

c2.title: Hosted in Europe
c2.desc: Our servers are at OVHcloud, in France. Subject to French law and GDPR. Your data never leaves the European Union.

c3.title: Open source, AGPL-3.0
c3.desc: Our entire codebase is public on GitHub. Audit it, submit improvements, or host your own instance.

c4.title: Zero ads, zero resale
c4.desc: We never serve ads. We never sell, share, or monetize your personal data or your files.

c5.title: Your files, not our fuel
c5.desc: Your data is never used to train AI models — neither ours nor third parties'.

c6.title: Minimal data collection
c6.desc: We only collect what is strictly necessary to operate the service. No hidden telemetry, no behavioral tracking.

values.sovSub: Digital sovereignty is not a single issue. It plays out on three distinct levels — and Kagibi addresses all three.

sov1.title: Where your data physically lives
sov1.desc: Geographic sovereignty refers to the physical location of the servers storing your data. Hosting outside Europe means exposing your files to foreign jurisdictions, regardless of the service provider's nationality.
sov1.b3: No replication outside the European Union

sov2.title: Under which law your data falls
sov2.desc: The US CLOUD Act (2018) compels American tech companies to provide data to US authorities, wherever it is stored in the world. Kagibi is a European entity, subject only to French law and GDPR.
sov2.b1: Immune to the US CLOUD Act
sov2.b3: No dependency on providers subject to foreign laws

sov3.title: Who actually holds your keys
sov3.desc: This is the most fundamental level: you alone hold the cryptographic keys that decrypt your data. Even if Kagibi were legally compelled to cooperate, we could provide nothing — we never have access to your keys.
sov3.b1: Encryption keys generated on your device
sov3.b2: Your password never leaves your browser
sov3.b3: Zero-knowledge: mathematically impossible for us to decrypt

values.manifestoQuote: The internet was supposed to set us free. Instead, it turned us into the product.
values.manifestoP1: Kagibi was born from a simple belief: your files, your conversations, and your personal data should never serve anything other than what you created them for. Not targeted advertising. Not AI model training. Not surveillance.
values.manifestoP2: We are not a startup looking to monetize your digital habits. We are developers who believe that privacy is a fundamental right — not a luxury reserved for tech-savvy users, not a checkbox in the fine print.
values.manifestoP3: That is why Kagibi is open source, hosted in Europe, and built on one principle: we cannot technically read your data. This is not a marketing promise. It is the architecture.
values.ctaSub: Take control of your data. Start for free, no commitment.

faq.heroSub: Clear answers about Kagibi's sovereignty, security, features and commitments.
faq.g1a: Kagibi is a sovereign, open-source cloud storage service built on a Zero-Knowledge principle. Your files are encrypted on your device before being sent — Kagibi technically cannot read your data.
faq.g2q: Where is my data physically stored?
faq.g2a: All your data is hosted exclusively on OVHcloud servers located in France (Roubaix and Gravelines). No replication outside the European Union is performed.
faq.g3q: Is Kagibi subject to the US CLOUD Act?
faq.g3a: No. Kagibi is a European entity hosted in France, subject only to French law and GDPR. The US CLOUD Act only applies to US-incorporated companies or those with servers in the United States — which is not our case.
faq.g4q: Is Kagibi free? How is it funded?
faq.g4a: Kagibi offers a free tier. Funding comes from paid subscriptions that provide more storage space. No personal data is sold or monetised for advertising purposes.
faq.g5a: Yes, entirely. Kagibi is published under the GNU AGPLv3 license — the strongest copyleft license. Any modification deployed in production must be made public. The code is freely auditable on GitHub.
faq.s1a: Zero-Knowledge means that encryption and decryption happen only on your device. Your password is never sent to the server. Kagibi receives only encrypted bytes — without your key, it is mathematically unable to read anything.
faq.s2a: No. This is a mathematical constraint, not just a promise. Files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM before leaving your browser. Kagibi never holds your password or decryption keys.
faq.s3q: What happens if I forget my password?
faq.s3a: During registration, you are given a 24-word recovery code. This code — and only this code — can unlock your account. Kagibi cannot reset your password on your behalf: no one holds your key, including us.
faq.s4q: Which cryptographic algorithms does Kagibi use?
faq.s4a: Kagibi uses AES-256-GCM for file encryption, Argon2id (64 MB RAM, 4 passes) to derive the key from your password, and RSA-OAEP 4096-bit for key sharing between users. All operations rely on the browser's native WebCrypto API.
faq.s5a: Kagibi offers optional file name encryption. When enabled, even metadata (names) is encrypted and unreadable by the server. By default, names may be visible in plaintext server-side to support certain features.
faq.cat3Title: What you can do with Kagibi
faq.f1a: P2P transfer lets you send files up to 10 GB directly from one browser to another, without going through our servers. The connection is established via WebRTC (with TURN relay if needed). Files are end-to-end encrypted during transfer and are never stored on our infrastructure.
faq.f2a: You can generate a sharing link for any file or folder. The recipient does not need a Kagibi account. The decryption key is included in the URL fragment (after the #), which is never sent to the server — only your browser can decrypt the file.
faq.f3q: What is direct sharing between Friends?
faq.f3a: The Friends list lets you add trusted Kagibi users. A direct share uses asymmetric cryptography: the file key is encrypted with the recipient's RSA public key, then sent. Only the recipient can decrypt — even Kagibi cannot intercept.
faq.f4a: Organisations let you create collaborative storage spaces for a team or company. Members of an organisation can share files in a common encrypted space with granular per-group permissions. Administrators can also synchronise members via LDAP/Active Directory.
faq.f5q: Can I access my files from multiple devices?
faq.f5a: Yes. Your Master Key is encrypted by your password and stored on the server. At each login, it is decrypted locally in your browser. You can therefore connect from any device and retrieve all your files.
faq.v1q: Does Kagibi use my data for advertising or commercial purposes?
faq.v1a: Never. Kagibi does not sell, rent, or analyse your data for commercial or advertising purposes. Our business model relies solely on paid subscriptions. Your files are cryptographically inaccessible to us anyway.
faq.v2q: Can I self-host Kagibi on my own infrastructure?
faq.v2a: Yes. Kagibi is designed to be self-hostable. The complete source code is available under AGPLv3. You can deploy your own instance on any compatible infrastructure, keeping full control over your data.
faq.v3q: Can Kagibi become proprietary or change its license?
faq.v3a: No, and this is a structural guarantee. The AGPLv3 license is irrevocable on already-published versions. Any future version remains covered by the same copyleft license, which prohibits creating closed proprietary forks. Kagibi formally commits to never switching to a more restrictive license.
faq.v4q: How does Kagibi make a lasting commitment to privacy?
faq.v4a: Privacy is Kagibi's architecture, not an option. In practice: client-side encryption by default, fully auditable open-source code, infrastructure in Europe, no data reuse, MFA available to all users, and a recovery code that ensures even under legal pressure on Kagibi, your files remain unreadable.
faq.ctaSub: Create a free account and explore for yourself — or dive into our security architecture for more depth.

gdImport.title: Import from Google Drive
gdImport.connectIntro: Connect your Google Drive account to import your files into Kagibi. The connection is strictly read-only.
gdImport.infoE2E: Each file is encrypted in your browser before being sent to Kagibi (zero knowledge).
gdImport.infoReadOnly: The requested access is read-only — Kagibi cannot modify or delete anything in your Drive.
gdImport.infoTokenNotStored: Your Google access token is never sent to or stored on Kagibi servers.
gdImport.infoWorkspace: Google Workspace files (Docs, Sheets, Slides) are converted to Office format (.docx / .xlsx / .pptx).
gdImport.errorNotConfigured: Google Drive import is not enabled on this instance.
gdImport.errorConnect: Unable to connect to Google Drive. Please check that you have granted access.
gdImport.dedicatedFolder: Import into a dedicated folder at Kagibi root
gdImport.foldersIntro: Choose the root folders to import from Google Drive. Subfolders and files they contain will be automatically included.
gdImport.conflictDesc: The folder "{name}" already exists at this location.
gdImport.conflictApplyAll: Apply this choice to all conflicts

step1.title: Welcome to SaferCloud
step1.desc: Here's a quick overview of what awaits you. Explore at your own pace.

step2.title: End-to-end encrypted storage
step2.desc: All your files are encrypted on your device before being uploaded. Even we cannot read your data.

step3.title: Collaborate as a team
step3.desc: Create or join organizations to share encrypted folders with colleagues or family.

step4.title: Real-time P2P transfer
step4.desc: Send files directly to a friend in real time, without going through our servers. Fast and secure.

step5.title: Share your files
step5.desc: Generate a secure sharing link for any file or folder. Protect it with a password or an expiration date.

step6.title: Protect your account
step6.desc: Enable two-factor authentication (MFA) in your security settings to strengthen your account protection.

done.title: You're all set!
done.desc: Discover all SaferCloud features and keep your files safe.
